content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

All three amendments identify real defects verified against the referenced code. fr-9-884f8134: config setters only invoke validate_iterative_minimal_settings which checks scope-internal ranges; the merged EffectiveConfig is only validated on load, so cross-scope mismatches persist to disk even though the setter returns an error (set_project:597 writes before load_for_project revalidates; set:570 passes ProjectConfig::default() entirely). fr-9-5a90e87a: invocation_context at 7781 emits only cycle/attempt/completion_round, and attempt_number at 7041 is cursor.attempt (constant across iterations); the loop at 6847 tracks `iteration` locally but only threads it into invocation_suffix for sidecar filenames, so the agent context is identical across all stabilization passes. fr-9-2345ceea: iterative_loop_exit_recorded at 6111 matches only stage/cycle/attempt/completion_round and does not inspect iteration or reason, so after SidecarUnavailable triggers a rewind (6437-6453) any stale ImplementerLoopExited from the abandoned terminal attempt blocks the new correct record at 6984 from being appended.

## Votes

- `fr-9-884f8134`: Accept
  Confirmed: set (line 570) calls validate_iterative_minimal_settings with ProjectConfig::default(), and set_project (line 597) only validates scope-internal ranges via validate_optional_iterative_minimal_range (lines 2240-2263), which short-circuits when either Option is None. The merged EffectiveIterativeMinimalPolicy validation (validate_effective_iterative_minimal_policy at 235) runs only on subsequent load_for_project calls — and in set_project the file is already written at line 598 before the final load at line 600 surfaces the error, leaving an invalid TOML on disk. Remediation to validate resolved policy post-edit is correct.
- `fr-9-5a90e87a`: Accept
  Confirmed: the iterative loop at line 6847 maintains a local `iteration` counter and passes it only as an invocation_suffix (line 6896 `it{iteration}`) used for sidecar file naming, never into the InvocationPayload.context. invocation_context (7781) emits only cycle/attempt/completion_round, and attempt_number at 7041 comes from cursor.attempt which is constant across iterations within a single stage attempt. The backend therefore receives identical request context on iteration 1 and iteration N, so the agent cannot adapt its behavior between first and stabilization passes. Threading iteration (and ideally max_rounds/stable_rounds_required) into the context is the right fix.
- `fr-9-2345ceea`: Accept
  Confirmed: iterative_loop_exit_recorded (6111-6127) only checks event_type + run_id + event_matches_stage_cursor (stage/cycle/completion_round) + event_matches_stage_attempt (attempt), without inspecting iteration or reason fields. When resume_terminal_iterative_stage_result hits SidecarUnavailable it rewinds state (6438-6453) and returns Reinvoke, but no existing ImplementerLoopExited event is cleared. The loop then re-runs iterations and at line 6984 the stale event suppresses the new correct append, leaving the journal pinned to whatever iteration/reason the abandoned attempt wrote. Either clearing stale events on rewind or comparing the existing event's iteration/reason against the current state is the correct fix.
